117922.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people who live in Leiden. With an equal number of men and women. Such a government includes different perspectives to make fair decisions.

117923.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

117926.explanation: We want one entertainment area where bars and clubs can extend their closing times. We do this in good consultation with residents. That way the city remains both lively and livable. We are open to new nightclubs and hospitality venues at suitable locations.

117927.explanation: While the national government has made a mess in recent years in refugee reception, we take responsibility in Leiden and provide humane shelter for people. We do not make things difficult about that in Leiden.

117931.explanation: In 2021, the municipal council already decided to gradually stop using terrace heaters at cafés and restaurants in Leiden.

117934.explanation: Events and traditions are part of Leiden, but we pay attention to balance. In Leiden, we have clear agreements about waste, noise, and lighting to minimize nuisance for residents and nature. We help events replace polluting machines with electric ones.

117939.explanation: Children sometimes face enormous pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is a way to relax. We want music lessons to be accessible not only for children with wealthy parents. With the Leidse Sleutelpas, we offer every child equal opportunities.

117943.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is important, but more cameras are not the right solution. Privacy is infringed and insecurity can simply shift elsewhere. That is why we opt for more enforcement officers on the street, also in the evenings, who can intervene immediately.

117945.explanation: At this moment, we do not take a position for or against the introduction of batons for enforcement officers (boa’s) in Leiden. We find it especially important that boa’s are well trained to deal professionally and calmly with aggression.

117946.explanation: We want starters (those who want to buy their first home) to have more opportunities. Young people from Leiden who want to live on their own often miss out now. They earn too much for social rent but too little to buy. That is why we also build extra mid-rent and affordable owner-occupied homes.

117947.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants first-time buyers or renters in Leiden to be given priority. We arrange this with housing corporations and project developers. In addition, we want people with crucial professions to have priority, such as police officers, healthcare workers, and teachers.

117950.explanation: The housing crisis affects everyone and requires a quick and effective approach from the municipality. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore wants vacant buildings to be better used. We also hold office building owners accountable for this.

117951.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to provide more affordable student housing, but it must also be pleasant to live in. We focus especially on mergers of student houses in central and accessible locations. This increases contact between students and helps combat loneliness.

117954.explanation: Where we as a municipality have influence, we must do everything to ensure our residents can live in security. We choose trust, customization, and justice. The Sleutelpas will be free for everyone with an income up to 150% of the social minimum.

117956.explanation: We already run such a trial in Leiden. There is a pilot that provides a basic income to a group of homeless and runaway youths in Leiden. This gives a group of homeless youths who were in a hopeless situation a new chance.

117957.explanation: The GroenLinks-PvdA alderman has already ensured that over 300 charging stations have been added. We monitor well whether that is sufficient. If more are needed, we think more should be installed.

117959.explanation: In Leiden, parking is sometimes free and sometimes paid. Our alderman introduced special short-term parking spots for those who want to shop quickly or pick up something. In the center, the regular tariff applies; outside the center, parking is cheaper.

117962.explanation: The current parking policy works. We have no plans to introduce paid parking in more neighborhoods.

117963.explanation: Everyone in Leiden must have access to appropriate care. Care is not about numbers or profit, but about people. About attention and proximity. We therefore want care in Leiden to be sensitive to differences between people, such as background, but also gender and sexual orientation.

117964.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest options. We must especially ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

117966.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA asks the strongest shoulders to carry the heaviest burdens. With the property tax (ozb) we can ensure that large companies fairly contribute to what the city provides. Over recent years, GroenLinks-PvdA in Leiden has shown how much is possible if we distribute the burdens fairly.

117969.explanation: We want to help green existing business parks and use the space there smarter. That creates a pleasant working environment, lowers costs for entrepreneurs, and makes a walk during the break much nicer.

117971.explanation: Only a very small part of the people who get priority for social rental housing are status holders. We believe that everyone who needs housing should be able to get one, and that no one in our city should needlessly have to sleep on the street.

117975.explanation: Thanks to our efforts, a modern music school will be established at this location. In one building, the Theater and the music school collaborate with partners from the city (such as music programmers) and all residents of Leiden have the chance to make music together, listen, and simply enjoy the atmosphere and music.

117977.explanation: Leiden has enough coffeeshops. We find it especially important that if people use drugs, it happens as safely as possible. For example, people must be able to test drugs anonymously, with attention to the risks of use.

117978.explanation: Over the past four years, GroenLinks-PvdA has already ensured at least 35% social rental housing in all construction projects. However, the great shortage of affordable homes requires more. That is why in the coming years we will aim for 40%. Everyone deserves an affordable and good home in a pleasant neighborhood.

117983.explanation: We will remove parking spaces that turn out to be unnecessary to create more space for playing, meeting, and living. This will be done in consultation with the neighborhood. We are investigating whether new parking garages are needed at the edges of the city center.

117984.explanation: We are making more parts of the city center car-restricted. Only destination traffic is allowed to drive there. This creates space for greenery, benches, cyclists, and pedestrians. We have been doing this for some time, and the experiences are positive.

117985.explanation: There is an enormous demand for housing and every vacant home feels like a waste. Since this summer, thanks to GroenLinks-PvdA, it is possible for the municipality to impose a fine on owners as a last resort.

117986.explanation: Together with other political parties, GroenLinks-PvdA has worked for years to continually expand the fireworks ban in the city. Moreover, a national ban is expected to come into effect next year. GroenLinks-PvdA wants the municipality to organize a large countdown party on New Year's Eve.

117922.explanation: D66 fights for emancipation and believes it is important that everyone has equal opportunities. We think it is important that girls in school and young women currently studying know they can achieve anything. A balanced distribution within the council is necessary for this.

117923.explanation: D66 stands for a governance culture where residents are involved from the beginning in major decisions. Citizen participation leads to better decisions and more support. Residents have knowledge and experience: let's use that! That is why we support referenda on clear questions.

117926.explanation: D66 works towards a lively city. We find the opening hours for hospitality venues and terraces too restrictive. In consultation with residents and entrepreneurs, we want to experiment to see if extending them is desirable. Closing times for nightclubs will also be extended in consultation.

117927.explanation: D66 wants people fleeing war and violence to always find safe shelter, including in the Leiden region. Leiden receives these people as agreed in the Distribution Act (Spreidingswet). We work with surrounding municipalities to realize this together.

117931.explanation: D66 recognizes that terraces and hospitality contribute to a cozy and lively city. We consider this important. Therefore, we are not necessarily against electric terrace heaters.

117934.explanation: D66 finds it important that the celebration of Leiden's Relief and festivals remain a party. Therefore, the noise limit until midnight will be raised by a few decibels. This allows stages further away to be heard well, prevents large crowds near the stage, and performances are not disturbed by background noise.

117939.explanation: A school day that also pays attention to sports, culture, and music offers a springboard for good development of children. D66 therefore wants every school to focus on this and subsidizes that each school can offer music lessons by a qualified music teacher.

117943.explanation: Everyone must be able to safely walk the streets. Cameras are currently installed in some city locations. Adding more cameras does not make the city safer because they often do not prevent crime. D66 prefers to address places perceived as unsafe (especially by women), for example through lighting and increased liveliness.

117945.explanation: D66 employs enforcement by special enforcement officers (BOA’s) to improve the living environment. They check, for example, on nuisance in parks, sexual harassment, and traffic safety. D66 believes that batons are not appropriate in this context. If violence must be used, it belongs to the police.

117946.explanation: D66 works for breakthroughs in the housing market. Only more houses will solve the shortage. We make housing sharing easier and build 10 new neighborhoods in the Leiden region, close to facilities and in green environments. We focus on more affordable housing – from social rental housing to owner-occupied homes.

117947.explanation: D66 focuses on 10 new neighborhoods with affordable homes. Especially new houses for starters, students, and elderly from Leiden and the region are needed. Through flow-through, homes become available for families and singles. People with essential professions, such as police officers and teachers, get priority.

117950.explanation: D66 works on 10 new neighborhoods in the Leiden region so everyone has a pleasant home. We build more houses and make sharing and subdividing homes easier. Subsidies are for cases where it does not succeed without government help. We do not convert offices where companies are looking for office space.

117951.explanation: D66 works in every way to create more homes so everyone has a pleasant place to live. Only more houses will solve the shortage. We build 10 new neighborhoods and make sharing and subdividing homes easier. Temporary (communal) student housing may be built on vacant sites.

117954.explanation: D66 advocates equal opportunities for everyone and wants municipal assistance to help Leiden residents achieve that. Unfortunately, there is such a thing as a “poverty trap”: the fact that if you earn just a bit too much, you fall outside all schemes. D66 wants to work with social organizations to provide a safety net here.

117956.explanation: D66 stands for personal freedom. Nationally, we advocate stopping benefits and a basic contribution for everyone. In Leiden, we replace all poverty schemes with one Participation Budget, with a separate child package for sports and support. This provides clarity and allows people to make their own choices.

117957.explanation: D66 wants all transport in the Leiden Region to become more sustainable. We commit to more charging facilities for electric transport. Think of cable trench tiles, charging stations in the curb, and charging light poles. We also want a study into reducing charging costs.

117959.explanation: D66 opposes introducing paid parking in Stevenshof, Zuidwest, Roomburg, and Merenwijk. In the city center, visitors park as much as possible in parking garages. This stimulates a level playing field for residents and entrepreneurs throughout the region.

117962.explanation: D66 opposes introducing paid parking in Stevenshof, Zuidwest, Roomburg, and Merenwijk. Visitors park as much as possible in a parking garage in the city center.

117963.explanation: D66 advocates for care nearby through low-threshold facilities where residents can just walk in. If desired, neighborhood teams provide support and help with care and welfare questions. We prioritize quality and proximity over uniformity of life philosophy.

117964.explanation: D66 strives for equal opportunities for everyone and wants youth care to help parents and children when they really need it. When choosing providers, we consider price, but also quality, effectiveness, and social value. Cheap must not be expensive in the long run.

117966.explanation: D66 does not want to raise property tax (OZB) now. D66 critically examines burdens on families, single-person households, and businesses. We demand a lot, which is only possible if we continue to invest in livability and a future-proof economy with the OZB, for now and for generations to come.

117969.explanation: D66 creates space for entrepreneurs to grow and innovate by literally making room on business parks throughout the region in cooperation with the regional development company. D66 also wants to build upwards to use the scarce space effectively.

117971.explanation: D66 wants people fleeing war and violence in the Leiden region to be housed according to the agreements in the Distribution Act (Spreidingswet). Once they have a residence permit, we ensure they can participate in society as soon as possible. We work with surrounding municipalities to realize this.

117975.explanation: Books, culture, and events provide relaxation and enjoyment. They offer opportunities for development and connection between people. Therefore, D66 wants a music school next to the Stadsschouwburg with practice rooms and a hall for performances so that everyone can enjoy learning music.

117977.explanation: D66 supports regulating rather than banning drugs. Coffeeshops should be able to operate transparently, legally purchase, sell, and deliver. Together with entrepreneurs, we try to prevent nuisance. As far as D66 is concerned, new coffeeshops may also open.

117978.explanation: D66 chooses affordable homes for young and old. In recent years, we have built over 8,500 homes and in the coming years will build another 11,000 homes, at least two-thirds of which will be affordable housing (social rental, mid-rent, and affordable owner-occupied homes for starters and seniors).

117983.explanation: D66 is greening the canals and moving cars to underground parking garages, for example under the Oude Vest. This frees up street space for greenery, benches, and liveliness. Together with residents and entrepreneurs, we seek practical solutions for loading and unloading. Street parking for professionals remains.

117984.explanation: D66 continues with the plan for a car-restricted city center. Within the canals, street parking will gradually disappear. Instead, parking garages will be built under the canals. Together with residents and entrepreneurs, we seek practical solutions for loading and unloading. Street parking for professionals remains.

117985.explanation: D66 works in every way to create more homes so everyone has a pleasant home. Only more houses will solve the shortage. We build 10 new neighborhoods in the region and make housing sharing easier. There is little vacancy in our city, and fines lead to procedures, not more houses.

117986.explanation: D66 supports a national fireworks ban for private individuals because too many people suffer unintended injuries. Emergency workers also deserve a safe New Year's Eve. We want the municipality to organize a communal fireworks show during the New Year.

117922.explanation: VVD Leiden supports diverse governance, but quality comes before quantity. Being equal is therefore undesirable, but worthy of pursuit.

117923.explanation: We have a representative democracy where participation can be involved through consultation. The VVD does not consider referenda a good alternative for this. However, we do believe in the right to challenge and good neighborhood participation, which must improve compared to current governance.

117926.explanation: The VVD believes there should be generous opening hours within which entrepreneurs have the flexibility to make their own choices.

117927.explanation: The influx of migrants must be reduced so that asylum reception in Leiden is not necessary. Until then, every municipality has a duty to accommodate asylum seekers and residence permit holders. The VVD wants to fulfill this but not take in more people than required.

117931.explanation: Terrace heating should be possible; it is up to entrepreneurs to make their own choice regarding this.

117934.explanation: The VVD wants to ease the permit regime for events to promote a lively city.

117939.explanation: The VVD does not consider this a municipal task. Choices need to be made.

117943.explanation: Everyone should be able to walk safely on the streets. That means a safe environment, visible presence of law enforcement officers, but also camera surveillance and better lighting in areas of the city that are unsafe, such as dark alleys.

117945.explanation: Our special enforcement officers (BOAs) deserve the right resources to ensure our safety. If necessary, they use batons to increase their own safety and that of our residents and emergency services in the city. Recent violent incidents against BOAs and emergency workers justify this.

117946.explanation: A starter’s loan can help but can also cause prices to rise further. To give first-time buyers a chance to find a home to buy, primarily affordable owner-occupied homes must be built. The VVD therefore wants at least 40% of new construction to consist of affordable owner-occupied homes.

117947.explanation: The VVD supports sufficient affordable rental and owner-occupied homes. To receive priority in social housing, it should concern urgent cases such as medical, psychosocial, or financial reasons.

117950.explanation: The VVD Leiden makes it easier for investors and developers to convert buildings. It is important that the municipality takes control and becomes more flexible. We want to get rid of rules, high costs, and slow procedures. But it is not necessary to provide subsidies for conversions.

117951.explanation: The VVD Leiden supports building more student housing, preferably through mergers by making agreements with DUWO and private parties during construction. This way, we can better manage scarce space and realize more homes. Temporary housing is an option where needed.

117954.explanation: The thresholds in Leiden are already quite high. Too often, the step from benefits to work barely pays off or not at all. Leiden’s schemes contribute (unintentionally) to this because they expire as soon as someone starts working.

117956.explanation: The VVD is against a trial with a basic income because it can discourage working, costs a lot of money, and its effectiveness is uncertain. We prefer targeted policies that help people find work and become self-reliant, instead of a generic income scheme.

117957.explanation: Supply and demand for charging spots must be well aligned. To make electric driving possible in Leiden, facilities for electric driving must be in order. We are experimenting with new rules so that, for example, cars may remain parked after they have been charged.

117959.explanation: The VVD is not in favor of paid parking. Only when there is extremely high parking pressure and residents themselves request paid parking can this be introduced. Parking rates are kept as low as possible.

117962.explanation: The VVD is not in favor of paid parking. Only when there is very high parking pressure and residents request it can this be implemented.

117963.explanation: People who need care must always be able to receive appropriate care. It is desirable if caregivers take clients' diversity into account, but this is not a right.

117964.explanation: Good, affordable youth care is important. The VVD supports a clear delineation of which problems belong in youth care; not every issue is for youth care. We also want to organize youth care as collectively as possible. Besides costs, we pay attention to quality and availability.

117966.explanation: The VVD believes that property tax (ozb) must be lowered because the burden on residents and entrepreneurs has increased sharply in recent years. The hard-working Leiden resident is not an ATM. The municipality must first make sharper choices and be more economical with tax money.

117969.explanation: Space for businesses must remain guaranteed. Agreed compensation for lost business parks will be honored. The VVD wants to speed up the development of the Vlietzone and the redevelopment of other business parks, compensate for lost space, and seek space for companies.

117971.explanation: The reception of status holders must be properly arranged, but this can also be in flexible housing. Mandatory priority for status holders does not fit because other people with urgency are also looking for housing.

117975.explanation: The plan already became almost three times as expensive as originally thought and we consider it too risky. The VVD values a good center for culture and music but wants it in a more logical location where a monumental building does not have to be demolished and it can be cheaper.

117977.explanation: At this moment, we see no reason for the arrival of a 12th coffeeshop in Leiden.

117978.explanation: The VVD wants at least 40% of the new construction to consist of affordable owner-occupied homes. This ensures flow through the housing market. Leiden already has relatively many social rental homes, at least 35% in new construction projects. Therefore, we want to lower the current 35% social rental homes in new construction to 30%.

117983.explanation: The car-free city center is complete. We will stop removing the 1,000 parking spaces in the city center that the current College has targeted.

117984.explanation: The car-free city center is complete. The city center must remain accessible for visitors from outside Leiden, for people with disabilities, and for larger purchases that require a car. Entrepreneurs are currently moving out of the city or considering stopping because they get fewer customers due to parking policies.

117985.explanation: Property owners usually handle their properties carefully and responsibly and make their own assessments of what is wise. The municipality must trust that and tackle vacancy by reducing rules and enabling redevelopment, not by imposing fines.

117986.explanation: Fireworks associations must, under conditions, be allowed to set off fireworks in neighborhoods so that people can safely enjoy our tradition close to home.

117922.explanation: We want a diverse and inclusive executive board, where everyone has a chance to be represented. Within the possibilities of the available candidates, we look beyond just the binary division between men and women.

117923.explanation: We support more direct influence from residents. With good information, they can think along about major decisions. The voices of animals, nature, and future generations also count. Depending on the topic, we choose the most suitable form, such as a citizens’ assembly or (neighborhood) referendum.

117926.explanation: Free opening times can cause more noise nuisance and unrest for neighbors and animals. The municipality must set frameworks to protect livability and nighttime rest, allowing room for customization when possible.

117927.explanation: Leiden must take responsibility for people fleeing their countries. We want small-scale, humane asylum reception spread across the city. With access to language, education, work, sports, and care, refugees can develop and participate in society.

117931.explanation: Terrace heating consumes a lot of energy and emits unnecessary CO2. What we do not consume, we do not have to generate sustainably. Therefore, we want to phase out terrace heating.

117934.explanation: Festivals belong to a lively city, but must take residents-both people and animals-into account. In some places this can be done well, in others not. With clear noise rules and customization, we ensure that celebrations remain possible without compromising livability and nature.

117939.explanation: Music education contributes to creativity, self-confidence, and equal opportunities. By making music lessons accessible to all children, regardless of income, we ensure every child can develop and discover talent.

117943.explanation: More cameras infringe on privacy and do not solve causes of insecurity. Camera surveillance must remain exceptional and temporary, under strict conditions.

117945.explanation: We want de-escalation and a safe public space but no further arming of special enforcement officers (BOAs). Training, prevention, and good cooperation with the police are more effective.

117946.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to help first-time buyers because housing is a basic right. But the starter’s loan is not the right instrument, as it drives up prices and saddles people with higher debts. The municipality must build affordable homes specifically for this group.

117947.explanation: Priority solely based on place of residence can disadvantage vulnerable groups. We choose social and fair allocation of housing, based on urgency and housing need.

117950.explanation: Empty offices are a waste of space. We prefer to make existing buildings habitable rather than building new ones, provided the renovation is sustainable and nature-inclusive.

117951.explanation: Students need housing quickly, and temporary flexible homes can provide relief in the current housing crisis. We also mainly want to invest in sustainable, full-fledged homes where students can live comfortably and long-term.

117954.explanation: Poverty does not stop at a strict income line. By raising the threshold, we reach more people who need support and prevent problems from worsening.

117956.explanation: A basic income can lead to more equality, security, and less stress and bureaucracy. It gives people space for caregiving, volunteering, and other valuable activities that often currently lack time or money. This can strengthen the wellbeing and the Leiden community as a whole.

117957.explanation: Electric driving is cleaner than fossil fuels. More charging spots with green electricity make switching easier, especially if we simultaneously focus on less car use and more public transport and cycling.

117959.explanation: Free parking seems attractive but encourages extra car use and more emissions. With more space for pedestrians, cyclists, public transport, and greenery, and affordable short-term parking with enough free spots for people with disabilities, shopping centers remain accessible and the surroundings pleasant and livable.

117962.explanation: We want the public space to be less dominated by cars. By focusing more on walking, cycling, and public transport, the city becomes safer, greener, and healthier. Parking permits help reduce parking pressure and make car-reduced neighborhoods possible, considering those who truly need a car.

117963.explanation: Care must be inclusive and accessible for everyone. The Party for the Animals supports culturally sensitive care that suits individual needs, but such needs must not lead to exclusion or discrimination against care providers.

117964.explanation: In youth care, quality and continuity must come first, not the lowest price. Market forces and price pressure have already caused enough damage.

117966.explanation: The municipality needs sufficient income for its public tasks and facilities, such as greenery, care, animal welfare, and poverty reduction. A lower property tax mainly benefits owners of expensive houses and leads to cuts. We prefer targeted exemptions for low incomes.

117969.explanation: Space in Leiden is limited. New business parks often come at the expense of green and natural areas and cause more traffic. We prefer to better utilize and make existing business parks more sustainable and allocate space for nature, housing, and livability.

117971.explanation: Refugees with residence permits need suitable housing quickly to settle safely. Like other urgent home seekers, they deserve support to participate in society within a fair housing policy.

117975.explanation: We support a strong cultural offer but a costly renovation of this cramped historic building is unwise. The new music school would be better placed somewhere with more space, for example outside the canals.

117977.explanation: We want a sensible coffeeshop policy with good distribution and clear rules. Not new shops everywhere, but also no total ban that would increase the street market.

117978.explanation: We want a high proportion of social rental housing in new construction, preferably at least 40%. This keeps Leiden affordable for people with low and middle incomes.

117983.explanation: Fewer parking spaces in the center provide room for greenery, clean air, and safe streets for people and animals. We allow residents to park in garages; visitors are encouraged to come by bike or public transport. There remains sufficient parking for people with disabilities.

117984.explanation: Where possible, we choose a car-free center with more space for pedestrians, cyclists, and greenery. Residents park in garages; visitors are encouraged to use bikes or public transport. The center remains accessible for people with disabilities and necessary destination traffic.

117985.explanation: Vacancy worsens the housing crisis and harms livability. With a vacancy fine, we encourage owners to reoccupy buildings or use them for social purposes.

117986.explanation: We want a complete ban on consumer fireworks. Fireworks cause much suffering to people, animals, nature, and the environment. We opt for a safe and festive New Year's Eve with more animal-friendly alternatives, such as a light or drone show, so everyone can enjoy without damage.

117922.explanation: Quality, support, and involvement in Leiden are paramount. Representation is good, but positions should not be distributed via fixed quotas.

117923.explanation: The city belongs to its residents, not the other way around. Leiden residents must have the final say through binding referenda and citizens’ initiatives. It must become easier to request a referendum.

117926.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve space, but opening hours must fit the environment. This requires tailored solutions and consultation with local residents to balance vibrancy and livability.

117927.explanation: Leiden must contribute in proportion to our size, available space, and level of facilities. Currently, we behave like the teacher’s pet. Party Sleutelstad was the only party in the council to vote against expanding the AZC in the Bockhorst.

117931.explanation: Party Sleutelstad opposes paternalism. Entrepreneurs can decide for themselves whether or not to install terrace heaters.

117934.explanation: This requires tailored solutions. Nuisance must be prevented, but parties should be possible. We must be careful not to make it impossible to organize anything in Leiden due to too many rules.

117939.explanation: For many children, the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture is the chance to participate in sports and cultural activities. Party Sleutelstad finds it essential that the municipality continues to support children from low-income families through this fund.

117943.explanation: Party Sleutelstad wants the municipality to expand camera use in places where things get out of control or where residents feel repeatedly unsafe. Deployment must be targeted and in consultation with local residents.

117945.explanation: Due to years of cuts to the police force, classic police tasks have increasingly ended up with enforcement officers (BOAs). Party Sleutelstad stands behind our enforcers. Where necessary for their safety, BOAs should be allowed to have defensive equipment.

117946.explanation: Many young starters do not qualify for social rent and are rejected for mortgages due to low income, temporary contracts, or student debt. Party Sleutelstad believes this balance must change and advocates for the introduction of a starter loan in Leiden.

117947.explanation: The housing shortage is enormous. Leiden residents must be able to keep living in their own city. Therefore, we advocate priority schemes for home seekers from the city itself.

117950.explanation: Transformation of offices into housing or mixed live-work areas is good, but the municipality is not here to enrich project developers. Only if a construction project financially does not work otherwise is this an option.

117951.explanation: This is not the municipality's responsibility but the University’s. The University must ensure there are enough rooms for (international) students instead of shifting this onto the city.

117954.explanation: Workers who just fall outside schemes also deserve support.

117956.explanation: Party Sleutelstad wants money for poverty policy to be directed specifically at Leiden residents. No random experiments.

117957.explanation: Facilitating electric driving is good. This must not come at the expense of parking facilities for regular cars.

117959.explanation: Grocery shopping must be affordable. We want parking at shopping centers to become free again or charged at a double tariff.

117962.explanation: The car is not a cash cow. We oppose expanding and splitting parking zones. There should be a single zone B again and paid parking in zone C should be abolished, except if a neighborhood referendum decides otherwise.

117963.explanation: The municipality must primarily ensure good, accessible, and affordable care. It is good to consider clients’ and patients’ wishes, but it is not the municipality’s responsibility to facilitate religion and lifestyles.

117964.explanation: The most important thing is that Leiden residents can use good, accessible, and affordable care.

117966.explanation: Leiden residents already pay more than enough. We want to end tax increases, including the property tax. Therefore: no increases higher than inflation.

117969.explanation: There must be sufficient space for entrepreneurs and businesses. More business parks must be added in the Leiden region.

117971.explanation: It is unfair that status holders get priority while Leiden residents who have to sleep in a garage, stay on friends’ sofas, live in moldy homes, or face other urgent situations have to wait eight years.

117977.explanation: Party Sleutelstad wants the municipality to take stricter action against companies causing nuisance in residential areas, such as shisha lounges and coffeeshops. We must be cautious about issuing new permits and strictly check for violations.

117978.explanation: Leiden residents must be able to live in their own city, even with a small or average income. Therefore we want 35% social rental housing, 30% affordable ownership, 20% mid-rent, and 15% free sector. Social rental homes must be allocated to housing corporations. No fake social rent.

117983.explanation: There must be enough parking for residents and visitors. We design public space in consultation with residents.

117984.explanation: Our priority is traffic safety and accessibility in all neighborhoods. We do not want prestige projects and ideological hobbyhorses that shift traffic problems to the suburbs.

117985.explanation: Vacancy must be countered. Especially in times of housing shortage.

117986.explanation: Party Sleutelstad is against a municipal fireworks ban. The capacity of the police and municipality should be used against troublemakers and fireworks vandals; not against families setting off some decorative fireworks.

117922.explanation: SVL strives to form a diverse executive board in all aspects. This applies both to the male/female ratio and background.

117923.explanation: It is now possible to request a referendum in Leiden. SVL wants to keep it that way. SVL focuses on democratic renewal, such as Democratie071, the youth council, citizen assemblies, children's elections, and the student and pupil debate.

117926.explanation: SVL wants to remove the restriction on current opening hours and believes that entrepreneurs should be able to decide themselves when no one is allowed in anymore. This will make Leiden's nightlife livelier.

117927.explanation: SVL believes that Leiden must contribute by being open to the reception of asylum seekers and status holders.

117931.explanation: Thanks to SVL, terraces in Leiden can stay open longer in the summer. SVL believes entrepreneurs should decide how their terrace is arranged. When it gets colder in the evening, terrace heating is pleasant.

117934.explanation: SVL supports a lively Leiden with a central entertainment area. SVL believes sound standards should be relaxed somewhat to encourage events and festivals in our city.

117939.explanation: SVL believes everyone should come into contact with culture. The municipality should facilitate the offer of cultural education. SVL advocates structurally restoring investment in the cultural development of youth.

117943.explanation: To guarantee residents’ privacy, cameras are only used for security purposes. Cameras work preventively and increase the feeling of safety. Cameras may only be deployed at carefully chosen locations and with clear communication.

117945.explanation: Thanks to SVL, enforcement officers in Leiden are now regularly trained to tackle street intimidation and inappropriate behavior. Additionally, SVL believes enforcement officers should receive extra powers to intervene properly.

117946.explanation: SVL aims for an inclusive housing market with opportunities for students, first-time buyers, and young people from Leiden. SVL believes this can be achieved by building extra homes and creatively managing existing housing. Only then will there be space again in Leiden’s housing market.

117947.explanation: SVL supports an inclusive housing market with equal opportunities for everyone. SVL wants to realize at least 35% social rental housing in new construction in Leiden, clearly striving for a home for all home seekers.

117950.explanation: We realize additional housing through subdividing, splitting, and new building. Being young should make starting a business and working in the city attractive. Office space in Leiden is scarce, so SVL supports preserving the existing office space.

117951.explanation: Temporary solutions do not solve the housing crisis. Building emergency housing may seem wise short-term, but these often stand for years in practice. SVL believes we must now invest in homes that permanently contribute to solving the housing shortage.

117954.explanation: Students are not covered by the municipality’s poverty scheme. As a result, students are excluded from municipal aid. By raising the income limit, we can also support this group in poverty.

117956.explanation: SVL wants youths who are homeless or living on the streets to receive monthly unconditional financial support. This support gives them peace and space to build their future.

117957.explanation: SVL supports freeing up parking spaces in the city center for greenery and terraces. Outside the city center, SVL wants parking garages that generate energy. This energy can be immediately used to charge electric vehicles by installing a battery.

117959.explanation: SVL wants to encourage as many people as possible to use sustainable transport. The car is seen as too burdensome. Short-term parking in the city center becomes more expensive, but parking permits outside the inner ring remain affordable.

117962.explanation: SVL wants short-term parking to be more expensive in the city center, but parking permits outside the inner ring to remain affordable. This encourages visitors to come by public transport or park outside the city center.

117963.explanation: Leiden must be an inclusive city where everyone feels welcome and there is no room for exclusion, hatred, or discrimination. This also applies to the healthcare sector.

117964.explanation: SVL supports a structural solution in youth care to tackle underlying problems. SVL advocates for better organized youth care with room for prevention and efficient care, where the child, not the price, is leading.

117966.explanation: Municipal taxes in Leiden are high. Waste collection fees, sewer and water charges, and property taxes cause residents to pay relatively high amounts. SVL strives to reduce this tax burden on residents where possible.

117969.explanation: Space in Leiden is scarce. Business parks are important for the local economy, but Leiden must also use space smartly, for example by realizing businesses on lower floors with housing above.

117971.explanation: SVL believes Leiden must contribute by being open to the reception of asylum seekers and status holders. Everyone entitled to social rental housing should be able to find a place.

117975.explanation: SVL is a strong supporter of a music school. The renovation at Hazewindsteeg is very costly and has a great impact on the Leiden Theatre. Alternative locations such as the Volkshuis are preferred by SVL.

117977.explanation: Coffeeshops fulfill a need for many Leiden residents. Opening a new location in Leiden should be possible under certain conditions.

117978.explanation: SVL supports a minimum social rental housing percentage of 35% in newly built homes so everyone can find a suitable and affordable home.

117983.explanation: SVL supports a car-reduced city center. SVL wants parking spaces in the city center to be replaced with greenery, bicycle parking, and terraces.

117984.explanation: SVL supports a car-reduced city center. There should only be space for destination traffic in the city center. SVL also wants to allow room for the shuttle bus to keep the city accessible to people with limited mobility.

117985.explanation: SVL supports an anti-vacancy ordinance in Leiden. Space in Leiden is scarce, and SVL wants all available space to be well used.

117986.explanation: SVL supports following national rules. New Year's Eve should be a celebration for everyone; SVL wants to ban fireworks from 2026 and invest in a central New Year's Eve celebration.

117922.explanation: Diverse teams perform better. This involves not only gender but also age and ethnic background. The CDA wants a diverse executive board but does not see an equal number of men and women as a strict condition.

117923.explanation: Difficult issues cannot be answered with a simple ‘yes’ or ‘no.’ The CDA is against referenda but supports citizen assemblies. We also believe citizen initiatives should have full space from local government.

117926.explanation: Cafés and restaurants also have a surrounding environment where people live and sleep, such as Leiden’s city center. A city center must not only be lively but also livable.

117927.explanation: The CDA stands for a generous and humane form of (temporary) shelter for those fleeing war and violence. The asylum center on Haagsche Schouwweg causes hardly any nuisance. The CDA wants Leiden to meet asylum seeker reception numbers according to the national dispersal law.

117931.explanation: The CDA is not against terrace heating but believes it should be done in as sustainable a way as possible.

117934.explanation: The sound standards in Leiden are already strict and that is good for the city's livability. Even stricter standards would make organizing events very difficult.

117939.explanation: The municipality does not need to subsidize everything for everyone. Parents can decide for their own child whether or not to take music lessons. For children in poverty, the CDA does advocate financial support.

117943.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety on the streets. In areas perceived as unsafe, such as the city center, the CDA wants more camera surveillance.

117945.explanation: Unfortunately, enforcement officers (BOAs) increasingly face aggression. The CDA believes they should be able to defend themselves against this, using pepper spray or, if necessary, a baton.

117946.explanation: To help young first-time buyers, the CDA opts for a 'Starters Guarantee Fund' for new-build homes. This can make housing more affordable. This fund offers the opportunity to bring homes to the market with discounts. When the home is sold, part of the surplus value is reimbursed.

117947.explanation: The CDA wants priority for (1) crucial professions such as police officers, teachers and nurses, and (2) within legal frameworks, for local home seekers. It is good that people whose lives revolve around the city have the greatest possible chance of owning their own home in Leiden.

117950.explanation: Thanks in part to CDA alderman Julius Terpstra, a lot is luckily being built in Leiden. To have a complete city, we also need entrepreneurs. Incidentally, there is hardly any vacancy in Leiden; there is more a lack of space for (local) entrepreneurs.

117951.explanation: The CDA sees no solution in temporary container homes for students and focuses on building more real and permanent student housing with common areas. The CDA wants to build large numbers of student homes in Leiden at the Praxis location, Westerpoort, and the Vrouwenweg monastery complex.

117954.explanation: The CDA wants working to pay off financially. A higher income limit means that people financially lose out when they start working because various poverty schemes stop. That cannot be the intention.

117956.explanation: It has been proven that a basic income does not make more people work. It does cause extra inflation and high costs.

117957.explanation: The CDA wants to make it easier for residents to charge their cars with their own solar power by legalizing and facilitating cable duct tiles, provided this can be done safely. The number of public charging points must also increase, but always in consultation with residents.

117959.explanation: At shopping centers where parking is currently free, it must remain so. At other shopping centers, the CDA wants to maintain the 'double rate'.

117962.explanation: The CDA does not want to expand paid parking where there is no problem. That means no paid parking in Stevenshof, Merenwijk, Waardeiland and Roomburg.

117963.explanation: The CDA is in favor of freedom of choice in healthcare, including when it is connected to religion, culture or belief. Care should fit who people are. At the same time, care must remain accessible, affordable and of good quality for everyone. Freedom of choice should never lead to exclusion.

117964.explanation: Children with problems deserve appropriate and proper care. That is the CDA's starting point. Youth care is too often a 'profit model' for providers. Therefore, the CDA wants a limited number of providers, stricter control of unnecessary costs and a cap on profits. Youth care is not a market.

117966.explanation: The CDA is against tax increases. In recent years, it has succeeded in keeping property tax (OZB) stable. For SMEs, the CDA wants to explore gradually lowering the property tax.

117969.explanation: There is a chronic shortage of industrial sites in Leiden. We want no more industrial sites to be lost; or they must be fully compensated. In the region, we are looking for additional space. Essential facilities, such as a hardware store, must be preserved for Leiden residents.

117971.explanation: The CDA supports priority for status holders in social rental housing because having a home is important for good integration. In addition, priority prevents long-term shelter in asylum seekers' centers. Of course, this must be combined with more housing construction and fair chances for all home seekers.

117975.explanation: The CDA wants further research first into the impact of this new cultural center both on the monumental monastery complex on this site and on the effects of a years-long construction project for the Schouwburg theater. A closure of the Schouwburg for years must be prevented.

117977.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce the number of coffeeshops so that supply matches local demand. Leiden does not need to be a drug supplier for the region.

117978.explanation: The housing stock in Leiden includes many social rental homes. To preserve the middle class, the CDA chooses in new-build projects for 25% social rental and 40% mid-segment, preferably owner-occupied homes.

117983.explanation: The city center is becoming low-traffic and many parking spaces have already been removed for this. The CDA does not want to remove any additional parking spaces.

117984.explanation: The city center is becoming low-traffic and the CDA supports that. Car-free is a step too far for us.

117985.explanation: The CDA is in favor of a vacancy tax because too many buildings stand unnecessarily empty while there is a high housing shortage.

117986.explanation: There will be a nationwide fireworks ban. The CDA supports a municipal fireworks show.

117922.explanation: The SP values equality between men and women. When equally qualified, decisions can be made based on the male/female distribution. The expertise and competence of people in these roles is crucial, and everyone is expected to act inclusively.

117923.explanation: The SP believes good participation must take place in advance of important decisions. We see a referendum as an important safeguard for residents.

117926.explanation: The SP values clear rules. The current closing times for cafés provide a good balance between nightlife enjoyment and peace in the streets. Therefore, the SP prefers to keep the current rules.

117927.explanation: The SP believes everyone fleeing war and violence has the right to dignified shelter. All municipalities should contribute jointly to this. The SP is proud there is support for this in Leiden as well.

117931.explanation: The SP thinks it is important to enjoy the outdoors, though it can be really chilly in cold Netherlands. However, it is important in the long term to make terrace heating more sustainable.

117934.explanation: The SP finds the current rules already strict. There should also be room for celebrations. It is more important to enforce realistically, with attention to various interests.

117939.explanation: The SP values cultural development opportunities for children. That is why the municipality subsidizes B+C. This also pays for music lessons. This way, we ensure that primary school children can enjoy music lessons, even if their household has a low income.

117943.explanation: The SP wants a safe Leiden. Therefore, more visible enforcement and police on the streets are needed. The SP wants less state surveillance and control over citizens and to respect privacy. Moreover, the SP wants to invest in society's quality and thereby prevent people from falling into crime.

117945.explanation: The SP believes BOAs must be able to do their work safely. That is why good cooperation with the police is necessary. A baton can escalate situations. The SP wants more visible police trained in combating serious crime. That reduces pressure on BOAs.

117946.explanation: The SP thinks housing must be affordable for everyone. We will not solve the overheated housing market with more loans that drive prices up further. The SP wants a fairer housing system and therefore more affordable housing to be built.

117947.explanation: At present, social rental homes are allocated regionally via the housing allocation system. The SP supports this. This way, scarce housing is distributed fairly and justly. Those who want can stay in the region and near family.

117950.explanation: The SP finds it important to make good use of existing space in the city. In some places, there is more need for housing than for office space. It is logical to switch according to demand, provided the distribution of costs is well-considered.

117951.explanation: Currently, there is a great shortage of student housing. As a result, students often pay too much for inadequate homes. The SP believes this must be solved sustainably. Meanwhile, temporary housing can alleviate the pressure.

117954.explanation: The line between just getting by and getting into trouble is too thin. Therefore, the SP believes timely help and support can prevent problems.

117956.explanation: At the SP's initiative, there is a pilot with a basic income for homeless youth. This has proven very successful. The current allowance system is too complex and mistrusts people. The SP wants a society based on trust.

117957.explanation: The SP believes there must be enough parking spaces for everyone dependent on a car. Electric driving is more sustainable than fossil fuels. Therefore, there must be enough charging points for electric cars.

117959.explanation: For the SP, parking is not a cash cow. It is important that facilities in neighborhoods remain accessible and affordable, also for people dependent on a car.

117962.explanation: For the SP, parking is not a cash cow. Only if a neighborhood itself requests more parking enforcement, for example to better spread parking pressure, is this possible for the SP.

117963.explanation: The SP finds it important that care is appropriate and respects Article 1 of the Constitution. Care providers must take into account the client's religious beliefs.

117964.explanation: Cheap is ultimately expensive. Tendering has made care more expensive and lowered quality. Treatments should mainly prevent problems. Profit-driven healthcare companies prefer to earn money rather than solve problems. This backfires.

117966.explanation: Municipal levies, such as property tax (ozb), are high in Leiden. But we get a lot in return; the level of amenities in Leiden is high. The SP does not want to raise the property tax unnecessarily.

117969.explanation: Land is scarce in Leiden, so we must make good use of space where the greatest demand is. There is a severe housing shortage. The SP believes that business parks fit better on the edges of the city.

117971.explanation: The SP believes that people fleeing war and violence should be accommodated with dignity. People with a residence permit have the right to live here. The pressure on the housing market is high. More building is necessary. Therefore, existing space must be allocated according to the greatest need.

117975.explanation: The SP values cultural development. Therefore, there must be enough space for it. By choosing a cheaper location, more money is available for quality and broadening the range.

117977.explanation: In Leiden, there are good agreements with coffeeshops, for example about opening hours and to whom they sell. The SP believes the municipality should carefully consider where coffeeshops are best located.

117978.explanation: The SP believes that everyone should be able to live affordably. The waiting lists for social housing are huge. Therefore, more social housing must be built. New construction should solve people's problems, not just enrich developers.

117983.explanation: The SP believes we must make the best possible use of space on the street. Fewer cars on the street make more attractive green and play facilities possible. Some people depend on a car. Therefore, the SP wants more underground or garage parking for residents.

117984.explanation: The SP believes the city center must remain accessible, including for people who depend on cars. A city center is safer and healthier with fewer cars. Therefore, the SP supports a low-traffic city center.

117985.explanation: The SP considers vacancy anti-social. Space in Leiden is scarce and we want a lot. Therefore, space must be used as optimally as possible.

117986.explanation: The SP believes we may celebrate the New Year festively, but fireworks are dangerous for humans, animals, and the environment. They also unnecessarily put pressure on our emergency responders.

117922.explanation: The ChristenUnie hopes that after the elections a council will be formed that addresses the problems of Leiden residents. An equal man-woman distribution within the council is desirable but not mandatory. This applies to gender, background, religious belief, etc.

117923.explanation: Referendums reduce complex choices to a simple 'for' or 'against.' As a result, interests are unreasonably exaggerated or ignored. The ChristenUnie wants to strengthen participation in the city. In participation projects, we work with different scenarios where citizens genuinely choose.

117926.explanation: The ChristenUnie does not want to expand the current number of hospitality venues and events in the city center. Nor do we want to extend terraces and closing times. The city center is primarily a residential area, where we want to protect livability.

117927.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes refugees must not suffer from failing policy. Therefore, we want the municipality to continue taking responsibility for asylum seeker reception. The ChristenUnie remains committed to ensuring the municipality meets its agreements.

117931.explanation: Terrace heating is often unnecessary and uses excessive energy.

117934.explanation: Leiden is one of the most densely populated and compact cities in the Netherlands. The balance between livability and vibrancy quickly comes under pressure. Stricter policies help protect the city's livability.

117939.explanation: Culture is important, but subsidies are not always the right tool. Therefore, the ChristenUnie wants a music bank where children can borrow instruments. We also make culture accessible for families up to 140% of the social assistance standard through generous arrangements.

117943.explanation: The ChristenUnie chooses cautious camera surveillance. Only where it demonstrably adds value, such as at the station and Papegaaisbolwerk. Expansion is currently unnecessary and may lead to false security and less capacity for other important enforcement.

117945.explanation: The ChristenUnie is supportive under conditions. The tasks of BOAs are becoming more complex, and they increasingly face unsafe situations. Therefore, we are positively critical of stronger equipment, provided it contributes to their safety and is used carefully.

117946.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes this is not a task for the municipality: the municipality is not a bank. Instead, we fully commit to affordable home ownership. Therefore, we want at least 50% of all new housing projects to be in the affordable segment.

117947.explanation: The regional housing regulation gives priority to the most vulnerable, such as homeless people, refugees, and women fleeing violence. Additional exceptions for Leiden housing seekers are unfair. We encourage turnover: seniors receive priority in the free sector and housing associations.

117950.explanation: Office vacancy in Leiden is very low, even lower than desirable. Therefore, it is simply not possible to convert more offices into homes. The ChristenUnie does want to focus more on housing above shops, where there is room to gain more homes.

117951.explanation: Leiden faces a large shortage of all types of housing, including for students. Space is scarce, but temporary solutions such as container homes, although challenging, can certainly help alleviate housing shortages and provide a quick solution.

117954.explanation: The number of working poor is growing, and aid organizations are warning more frequently. Because sometimes receiving social assistance is more advantageous than working, the ChristenUnie wants to support this group well with generous arrangements for people with incomes up to 140% of the social assistance standard.

117956.explanation: The municipality already provides various workplaces for those who cannot do regular work. A pilot with a basic income for homeless people is good and important, but further development of a basic income is the responsibility of the national government, not the municipality.

117957.explanation: The municipality ensures sufficient charging points that meet demand. We also promote the installation or allowance of cable ducts in Leiden, making charging electric vehicles easy and accessible for everyone.

117959.explanation: Parking spaces are disappearing, and paid parking is the norm, but this mainly affects people with lower incomes, the elderly, practical workers, and people with disabilities. The ChristenUnie wants social parking policies, where close parking remains a necessity and parking is not a profit goal.

117962.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants social parking policies. Paid parking mainly affects people with low incomes, the elderly, practical workers, and socially vulnerable groups. Close parking is not a luxury; therefore, we want no paid parking in Merenwijk, Roombrug, and Stevenshof, and free parking remains on Sundays.

117963.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes it is important that residents can freely choose the care they need. Trust in caregivers is essential for quality care. Therefore, the Personal Budget (PGB) must not be displaced by Care in Kind (Zorg in Natura).

117964.explanation: The municipality must select everything based on quality. Of course, costs must be carefully weighed, but the risk of cheap care is that we will pay the price for it in the future.

117966.explanation: The property tax (OZB) has been used as a cash cow to finance all kinds of plans in recent years by the city government. The ChristenUnie is very critical of this because you must first be critical of your own expenses. Local levies, in our view, should not be raised more than the general trend.

117969.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants smarter collaboration with municipalities in the region to efficiently allocate business parks by function. There is no room for additional sites in Leiden, so a robust regional approach is needed to optimally use business activity and space.

117971.explanation: The pressure on reception is high, but refugees must not suffer from failing national policy. The ChristenUnie wants the municipality to continue taking responsibility and to strive to meet the agreements of the Distribution Law for asylum seeker reception.

117975.explanation: Leiden urgently needs a new music school, but not at the current complex location. The ChristenUnie chooses a pragmatic approach, puts users and teachers first, and focuses on quality and accessibility. The Hazewindsteeg has become too much a prestige project.

117977.explanation: Leiden must be a healthy city, especially for young people. As far as we are concerned, there is no room for coffeeshops in the city. Therefore, we want to significantly reduce the number of coffeeshops. Leiden has an above-average number, and they cause much nuisance for local residents.

117978.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants at least 35% social rental and 50% mid-segment homes (purchase/rental) in future new construction projects. By setting clear requirements, Leiden will be accessible to young people, families, and people with lower incomes.

117983.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants social parking policies: parking must be accessible to everyone and not aimed at profit. There are many parking garages in the center, so surplus spaces can be removed and green areas created, but this must be well explained to local residents.

117984.explanation: The Christian Union wants to discourage unnecessary car traffic because cycling is healthier. However, people who depend on cars must still be able to reach the city center. Therefore, we maintain the policy of 'bike over car' and a 30 km/h speed limit throughout the city.

117985.explanation: The municipality has policies to prevent housing vacancies. Owners who structurally refuse to rent out or sell a vacant home will be penalized.

117986.explanation: From next year, there will be a nationwide fireworks ban. The municipality of Leiden enforces this national fireworks ban consistently. The Christian Union has always advocated for a city festival with a central fireworks show on New Year's Eve.

117922.explanation: Volt wants parity, meaning equal representation and inclusion. Diversity in the executive board strengthens decision-making and ensures Leiden is better governed from various perspectives, experiences, and talents.

117923.explanation: Volt is against referenda. However, we believe in democratic renewal through citizens’ assemblies and digital participation. These offer residents more influence than referenda, which often oversimplify complex issues.

117926.explanation: Entrepreneurship is important but must be balanced with livability. Volt Leiden chooses tailor-made solutions where opening hours fit the neighborhood and nuisance for residents is avoided.

117927.explanation: Volt stands for humane and responsible reception of people fleeing conflict. Leiden must (as always) show solidarity, provide good guidance, and contribute to national solutions rather than reducing reception capacity.

117931.explanation: Terrace heating is energy wasteful and does not fit a sustainable city. Volt chooses smart alternatives and supports hospitality businesses in transitioning to climate-friendly solutions.

117934.explanation: Volt supports a vibrant cultural life but wants to protect health and livability. Tailored solutions per location and time are better than generic loosening of noise standards.

117939.explanation: Volt believes every child has the right to music lessons, but parents who can pay themselves may be asked for a contribution. Another way to ensure every child receives music lessons is to include 'music' in the curriculum.

117943.explanation: Volt stands for a safe Leiden. Camera surveillance increases the feeling of safety in the city and can help prevent crime and violence. Volt advocates targeted camera surveillance in places where insecurity is often experienced. The condition is that privacy is guaranteed.

117945.explanation: Volt is cautious on this. Enforcement officers have a civil role. De-escalation, training, and cooperation with the police contribute more to safety than further arming.

117946.explanation: Volt wants to give first-time buyers a fair chance in the housing market. A starter loan helps young people in Leiden and those with middle incomes to buy their first home without taking irresponsible financial risks.

117947.explanation: Volt believes social rental homes should be accessible to those who need them most. Inclusivity and equality come first. Rigid priority based on place of residence undermines solidarity and increases inequality among regional housing seekers.

117950.explanation: Volt sees repurposing as a smart solution to the housing shortage. With targeted subsidies, vacancy can be reduced and sustainable living space quickly added without extra land use.

117951.explanation: Volt sees temporary student housing as a necessary complement to structural solutions. It offers quick relief to the housing market, provided the homes are of good quality and affordable.

117954.explanation: Volt wants to better reach the group of 'working poor' in Leiden. By raising the income limit, more people receive timely support and debt and stress are prevented.

117956.explanation: Volt supports expanding the successful 'Building Deposit' pilot program that Leiden started for young people in vulnerable positions. Volt supports a basic income and sees this pilot as an interesting benchmark.

117957.explanation: Leiden promotes the use of electric cars because they are cleaner. More electric charging stations are necessary for this.

117959.explanation: In shopping centers outside the city center where there is enough space, parking can remain free. This benefits consumers, helps entrepreneurs as shops remain easily accessible, and prevents parking pressure in neighborhoods.

117962.explanation: Parking policy requires tailor-made solutions. Volt believes that in neighborhoods without parking congestion, permits should not be required.

117963.explanation: Volt believes care should be arranged as much as possible tailored to needs but also that care providers should not be refused based on culture or origin. Volt considers care a basic right. At the same time, there is a shortage of care. Unfortunately, not every resident can get a care provider of their choice.

117964.explanation: Good care for our youth must come first. Volt believes the municipality should primarily focus on the quality of youth care. Only after that should cost be considered.

117966.explanation: To enable social investments, for example in housing and climate, taxes must be levied. Volt believes this must be done responsibly, fairly, and purposefully.

117969.explanation: Leiden itself has little space for more business parks. The available space is also needed for housing. However, Volt aims to engage in talks with surrounding municipalities (Leiderdorp, Katwijk, Oegstgeest) about housing for SMEs and startup hubs that want to expand.

117971.explanation: We have a moral duty to protect refugees and affordable housing is part of that. Volt opposes prolonged stays in asylum seeker centers for asylum seekers with residency status. These people must be helped as quickly as possible with housing and work or education.

117975.explanation: Volt encourages culture but feasibility of this project must be carefully considered.

117977.explanation: Location, safety, and nuisance must be taken into account. Currently, the problem is that many coffeeshops are clustered in the city center, while there are hardly any in other neighborhoods or surrounding municipalities. Volt supports dispersion: fewer in the city center, more elsewhere.

117978.explanation: Volt advocates for at least 30% social housing but certainly not fewer affordable owner-occupied homes. More owner-occupied homes must also be built because only with affordable owner-occupied homes can there be housing flow.

117983.explanation: The city center must offer more space to pedestrians and cyclists. However, the municipality must ensure sufficient parking spaces in parking garages both in the center and outside. Incidentally, Volt believes Leiden is doing well in this.

117984.explanation: Volt wants a low-car city center. Deliveries for shops and businesses with small and clean vans in the center must remain possible, but alternatives can be considered, such as transport via waterways. Volt will look at other European cities with historic centers, such as Bilbao.

117985.explanation: There is a severe housing shortage in Leiden. It is irresponsible to leave living space unused but also to leave large office buildings empty. Volt wants the municipality to be able to take measures to hold owners accountable.

117986.explanation: A clear decision has been made by the House of Representatives: a fireworks ban will be implemented. Volt supports this. Fireworks cause pollution, frightened pets, and unnecessarily burden emergency services. Incidentally, Volt supports a fireworks show organized by the municipality, like on October 3.

statements.title: Leiden should run a pilot program for a basic income.

titleParts.text: Leiden moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The residents of Leiden should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Leiden moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Leiden should increase CCTV surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Leiden should introduce parking permits in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Leiden moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The center of Leiden should become car-free.
The BOAs who enforce safety in Leiden should be issued a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Leiden controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Of the new homes to be built in Leiden at least 35% should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rentals.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Leiden moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: More business parks should be developed in Leiden.
There should be fewer parking spaces in the centre.
The municipality should continue to allow the setting off of fireworks on New Year's Eve.
Leiden should build a new cultural center at Hazewindsteeg 2-4.
Parking at shopping centers must remain free.
The municipality should fine owners who leave their buildings vacant.
